perturbation.original_text: The following terms are defined for the purposes of this Agreement as follows: (a) "Acceptance" shall mean MBE's acknowledgment pursuant to Section 2 ---------- below that (i) access to the Service has been granted, and (ii) the Service functions in accordance with the Specifications.
perturbation.changed_text: The following terms are defined for the purposes of this Agreement as follows: (a) "Acceptance" shall mean MBE's acknowledgment pursuant to Section 2 ---------- below that (i) access to the Service has been granted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the definition of 'Acceptance'. The original definition required both access to the Service and its proper functioning according to Specifications. By removing '(ii) the Service functions in accordance with the Specifications', Acceptance is now solely based on access, regardless of whether the Service actually meets the Specifications. This contradicts section 2(d) which states 'Upon verification by MBE that the Manifest conform with the Specifications in all material respects, or if MBE fails to notify the Company within the twenty (20) business day inspection period, the Service shall be deemed Accepted.', thus creating uncertainty as the service could be 'Accepted' even if it does not conform with specifications, due to the perturbed defintion of acceptance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Acceptance. Upon verification by MBE that the Manifest conform with the ---------- Specifications in all material respects, or if MBE fails to notify the Company within the twenty (20) business day inspection period, the Service shall be deemed Accepted. MBE's sole remedy for correction of problems after Acceptance shall be under the Warranties set forth in Section 11.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Acceptance', removing the requirement for the Service to function according to specifications. This directly contradicts section (d) which states that Acceptance is based on verification that the Service conforms to the specifications. The modified definition allows for Acceptance even if the service doesn't meet specifications, creating a clear in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the acceptance criteria, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: (d) Customer Information. The Company and MBE shall jointly own (without -------------------- duty to account) all customer lists and related customer data gathered through the use of the Service by customers of MBE and MBE Centers ("Customer Data"). ------------- During the term of this Agreement, neither party shall provide any Customer Data to any third party (other than MBE Centers or an affiliate of MBE or the Company) without the prior written consent of the other party The Company shall initially provide the Customer Data to MBE in acceptable electronic format as soon as practicable following the reasonable request of MBE, and, once MBE (at its sole cost and expense) has implemented an appropriate means to warehouse the Customer Data, the parties shall take reasonable steps to transfer all existing Customer Data to MBE and implement a system whereby the Customer Data is automatically downloaded and provided to MBE on a periodic basis. The Company agrees to retain the Customer Data in accordance with the Company's own data retention policies, and in any case for no less than 13 months from the creation of such Customer Data. The Company, MBE, affiliates of the Company and MBE, and the MBE Centers may each use the Customer Data for marketing and other purposes in accordance with a mutually agreed upon privacy policy and in accordance with any mutually agreed upon privacy policy displayed to customers in connection with the Service. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company agrees not to use any Customer Data to solicit any MBE-Generated Customers with respect to any services offered by the Company; provided, however, that the Company may use Customer Data to solicit such customers who have a shipping account with a carrier or with respect to services not offered by MBE and the MBE Centers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, MBE agrees not to use any Customer Data in any manner which would reasonably be expected to eliminate any Bounty Package payment to the Company hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: (d) Customer Information. The Company shall exclusively own (without -------------------- duty to account) all customer lists and related customer data gathered through the use of the Service by customers of MBE and MBE Centers ("Customer Data"). ------------- During the term of this Agreement, the Company may provide any Customer Data to any third party without the consent of MBE. The Company shall initially provide the Customer Data to MBE in acceptable electronic format as soon as practicable following the reasonable request of MBE, and, once MBE (at its sole cost and expense) has implemented an appropriate means to warehouse the Customer Data, the parties shall take reasonable steps to transfer all existing Customer Data to MBE and implement a system whereby the Customer Data is automatically downloaded and provided to MBE on a periodic basis. The Company agrees to retain the Customer Data in accordance with the Company's own data retention policies, and in any case for no less than 13 months from the creation of such Customer Data. The Company, MBE, affiliates of the Company and MBE, and the MBE Centers may each use the Customer Data for marketing and other purposes in accordance with a mutually agreed upon privacy policy and in accordance with any mutually agreed upon privacy policy displayed to customers in connection with the Service. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company agrees not to use any Customer Data to solicit any MBE-Generated Customers with respect to any services offered by the Company; provided, however, that the Company may use Customer Data to solicit such customers who have a shipping account with a carrier or with respect to services not offered by MBE and the MBE Centers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, MBE agrees not to use any Customer Data in any manner which would reasonably be expected to eliminate any Bounty Package payment to the Company hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction regarding Customer Data ownership and usage restrictions. Originally, Customer Data was jointly owned, and neither party could provide it to third parties without consent. Now, the Company exclusively owns the data and can share it without MBE's consent. However, the clause 'The Company, MBE, affiliates of the Company and MBE, and the MBE Centers may each use the Customer Data for marketing and other purposes' still remains which creates an internal contradition if the company exclusively owns the data, why it would need to include MBE and affiliates of the company and MBE to use the data? This pertubation contradicts the following sentence, creating the ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Customer Information. The Company shall exclusively own (without -------------------- duty to account) all customer lists and related customer data gathered through the use of the Service by customers of MBE and MBE Centers ("Customer Data"). ------------- During the term of this Agreement, the Company may provide any Customer Data to any third party without the consent of MBE. The Company shall initially provide the Customer Data to MBE in acceptable electronic format as soon as practicable following the reasonable request of MBE, and, once MBE (at its sole cost and expense) has implemented an appropriate means to warehouse the Customer Data, the parties shall take reasonable steps to transfer all existing Customer Data to MBE and implement a system whereby the Customer Data is automatically downloaded and provided to MBE on a periodic basis. The Company agrees to retain the Customer Data in accordance with the Company's own data retention policies, and in any case for no less than 13 months from the creation of such Customer Data. The Company, MBE, affiliates of the Company and MBE, and the MBE Centers may each use the Customer Data for marketing and other purposes in accordance with a mutually agreed upon privacy policy and in accordance with any mutually agreed upon privacy policy displayed to customers in connection with the Service. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company agrees not to use any Customer Data to solicit any MBE-Generated Customers with respect to any services offered by the Company; provided, however, that the Company may use Customer Data to solicit such customers who have a shipping account with a carrier or with respect to services not offered by MBE and the MBE Centers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, MBE agrees not to use any Customer Data in any manner which would reasonably be expected to eliminate any Bounty Package payment to the Company hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text establishes that the Company exclusively owns the Customer Data and can share it with third parties without MBE's consent. However, the subsequent clause, 'The Company, MBE, affiliates of the Company and MBE, and the MBE Centers may each use the Customer Data for marketing and other purposes', contradicts this exclusive ownership. If the Company exclusively owns the data, it shouldn't need to explicitly include MBE and its affiliates in the permission to use the data. This creates a clear contradiction, making it unclear who has the right to use the data for marketing purposes. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' where key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: The parties further agree that NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, FOR COSTS OF PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES OR FOR ANY CLAIM OR DEMAND AGAINST A PARTY BY ANY OTHER PARTY. IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR Source: STAMPS.COM INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, INDIRECT, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF A PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES, AND UNDER ANY CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE. THESE LIMITATIONS SHALL APPLY NOTWITHSTANDING ANY FAILURE OF ESSENTIAL PURPOSE OF ANY LIMITED REMEDY.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties further agree that NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, FOR COSTS OF PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES OR FOR ANY CLAIM OR DEMAND AGAINST A PARTY BY ANY OTHER PARTY. IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR Source: STAMPS.COM INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF A PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES, AND UNDER ANY CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE. THESE LIMITATIONS SHALL APPLY NOTWITHSTANDING ANY FAILURE OF ESSENTIAL PURPOSE OF ANY LIMITED REMEDY.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by selectively limiting the types of damages for which neither party will be liable. The original text excludes liability for 'CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, INDIRECT, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES.' By removing 'INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, INDIRECT, OR EXEMPLARY', the amended text suggests that only consequential damages are excluded. However, this creates uncertainty because Section 13 explicitly discusses indemnification for damages, and if those damages are no longer limited, that creates an ambiguity on whether those indemnifications are subject to special indirect, or exemplary damges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Indemnification by the Company. The Company, at its own expense, will ------------------------------ indemnify, defend and hold harmless MBE, and its employees, franchisees, representatives and -15- agents, against any claim, suit, action, or other proceeding brought against MBE or such party, to the extent that such claim, suit, action or other proceeding is based on or arises from. (i) any misrepresentation or breach or representation or warranty of the Company contained herein; or (ii) any breach of any covenant or agreement to be performed by the Company hereunder. The Company will pay all costs, damages, and expenses, including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees and costs awarded against or otherwise incurred by MBE in connection with or arising from any such claim, suit, action or proceeding attributable to any such claim.
perturbation.justification: The original text broadly excludes liability for consequential, incidental, special, indirect, or exemplary damages. The changed text removes the exclusion for incidental, special, indirect, or exemplary damages, thereby creating a contradiction with the indemnification clause. The indemnification clause requires the company to pay "all costs, damages, and expenses". This is now interpreted to include incidental, special, indirect, or exemplary damages, where the original text prevented such an interpretation. Therefore, the change introduces a clear and direct conflict within the document, rendering it ambiguous and contradicting the indemnification requirements.
